node.statement: (Automatic purity theorem). If the face dimension gap satisfies G(A) ⊇ {1, 2, ..., ℓ-1} (i.e., the smallest positive face dimension in S(A) is ≥ ℓ), then for any set C of states defined by m ≤ ℓ-1 affine constraints, every extreme point of C is a pure state (extreme in S(A) itself). Proof: Let φ be extreme in C. By the main theorem, dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≤ m ≤ ℓ-1. But dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ∉ {1, ..., ℓ-1} by the gap hypothesis. Since dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) ≥ 0, the only possibility is dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = 0, i.e., φ is pure.
